content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 3, 1992


Michael B. Bixler
Mayor of City of Imperial Beach
1192 Seacoast Drive
Imperial Beach, CA 91932

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-92-378

Dear Mr. Bixler:

I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation of
June 30, 1992, wherein we discussed your request for advice,
Letter No. I-92-378. As the Mayor of the City of Imperial Beach,
you were requesting advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since you
have not provided specific facts regarding each decision, we are
providing informal assistance.²

You informed me that there are approximately ten decisions
regarding a number of public accesses to the beach and also a
series of decisions regarding the City of Imperial Beach Capital
Improvement Project. Your home is approximately 300 to 2,500 feet
from the subject properties of each decision.

We discussed Regulation 18702.3(a)(3) and whether there would
be an effect of $10,000 or more on the fair market value of your
property as a result of any of the decisions. You stated that, in


1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. I-92-378
Page 2


your opinion, such an effect was not foreseeable.  I advised you
that this is a factual determination which you must make for each
pending decision.  I also directed you to the factors in
Regulation 18702.3(d) for guidance.  (Regulation 18702.3
enclosed.)

I trust this answers your question.³

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        By: Jill Stecher
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JS:aa

Enclosure




³ Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question_synthetic: As Mayor of Imperial Beach, does the requestor have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding approximately ten decisions about public beach access and a capital improvement project, given that his home is 300-2,500 feet from the affected properties?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that whether there would be a $10,000 or more effect on the fair market value of the requestor's property is a factual determination the requestor must make for each pending decision, and directed the requestor to Regulation 18702.3(d) for guidance on the relevant factors.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter with no explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; advice conveyed through discussion of telephone conversation and factual determination requirements. Note that informal assistance does not provide immunity unlike formal opinions.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: As Mayor of Imperial Beach, does the requestor have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding approximately ten decisions about public beach access and a capital improvement project, given that his home is 300-2,500 feet from the affected properties?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised that whether there would be a $10,000 or more effect on the fair market value of the requestor's property is a factual determination the requestor must make for each pending decision, and directed the requestor to Regulation 18702.3(d) for guidance on the relevant factors.

embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter to the Mayor of Imperial Beach regarding potential conflict-of-interest implications of his decisions affecting properties near his home. The Commission clarified that the requestor must determine on a case-by-case basis whether decisions would result in a $10,000+ impact on his property's fair market value.
